title: Crunchy Charred Venetian Napa Cabbage Cassata
description: A spicy and flaky Italian dish featuring Napa cabbage, smoke, and high protein, wrapped in pandan leaves. This adventurous cassata is perfect for those looking for a unique and savory meal.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large skillet, cook the Italian sausage over medium-high heat, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks.
Add the chopped prosciutto to the skillet and cook until crispy.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked sausage and prosciutto, shredded Napa cabbage, ricotta cheese, Parmesan cheese, salt, black pepper, and red pepper flakes.
Lay a pandan leaf flat on a work surface and place about 1/6 of the cabbage mixture in the center of the leaf.
Fold the leaf over the filling to form a triangle or square shape, and repeat with the remaining leaves and filling.
Place the cassata on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, brush with olive oil, and bake for 25 minutes.
Increase the oven temperature to broil and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, or until the leaves are charred and crispy.
Remove the cassata from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
